question: Can a split-source HIP tile-segment D-block reducer make compressed dKV group-aware enough to fold into the no-HSK sparse-MLA backward boundary?

compile_result.reason: Using torch.utils.cpp_extension.load with separate binding.cpp and kernel.hip prevents load_inline from injecting torch/types.h into the HIP translation unit.
compile_result.old_failure: load_inline prepended torch/types.h to hip.hip, triggering ROCm 7.2 Thrust type_traits/type_traits_hip duplicate definitions.

read: The file-based split HIP build is a useful compile fix, but this scalar tile-segment D-block reducer is not a viable sparse-MLA backward path. It reduces modeled global writes by 23.8x, yet serializes too much contribution work inside each segment/D-block CTA and lands around 116 ms, about 13.2x slower than the existing sparse atomic wall.

decision.keep_compile_lesson: For HIP custom probes in this image, prefer file-based load() or a real extension build over load_inline() when the HIP translation unit must avoid Torch/Thrust headers.
decision.next_sparse_mla_path: Port the whole sparse-MLA backward boundary with query-major recompute and a group-aware epilogue. Keep q/dO reuse hot, avoid HSK P/scaled-dP slabs, and reduce by compressed group inside the producer epilogue without making group/segment ownership serialize the MFMA contribution loop.

materialized_e2e_tflops.reason: The isolated reducer failed the S4096 sparse-atomic latency gate by a large margin, so an e2e TorchTitan A/B would chase a known-negative branch.
